content.summaryText: Generalized Anxiety Basics in Anxiety Spectrum Disorders focuses on symptom pattern recognition and functional impairment assessment. Primary objective: Recognize persistent worry patterns.

content.questionText: In Generalized Anxiety Basics, which action best supports 'Recognize persistent worry patterns'?
content.explanation: Correct answers prioritize patient safety, reassessment, and timely escalation.

options.text: Establish safety, rule out medical mimics, and use calm communication
Labeling all somatic symptoms as psychological without clinical screening
Delay reassessment until symptoms are severe
Use treatment decisions without baseline documentation

content.summaryText: Immediate priority: establish safety, rule out medical mimics, and use calm communication. Practical emphasis: Differentiate normal stress.

content.questionText: Escalation is required when severe distress, suicidality, substance risk, or inability to self-care.
content.explanation: Early escalation reduces preventable deterioration and delays in definitive care.

content.sentence:
Start with <1> to establish baseline status.
Use <2> to deliver standardized intervention.
Document <3> to support continuity and quality review.
